title: Way Too Long Words
description: Sometimes some words like "localization" or "internationalization" are so long that writing them many times in one text is quite tiresome. So let's write such words in a special abbreviated way. We replace the letters between the first and the last letter with the number of those letters. For example, "internationalization" becomes "i18n", and "localization" becomes "l10n". Write a program that shortens words in this way. A word is only abbreviated if it is strictly more than 10 characters long.
input_spec: The first line contains an integer n (1 <= n <= 100). Each of the following n lines contains one word. All words consist of lowercase Latin letters and are at most 100 characters long.
output_spec: For each word, print its abbreviation if the word has more than 10 characters, or the word itself otherwise.

routes.name: Direct string manipulation
routes.description: Check the word length and construct the abbreviation from the first letter, middle count, and last letter.

observations.title: Identify when to abbreviate
observations.description: A word is only abbreviated when its length is strictly greater than 10. Otherwise, it is printed as-is.

hints.nudge: Re-read the problem statement -- when exactly should you abbreviate?
hints.question: What is the length threshold? Is a 10-character word abbreviated or not?
hints.formal: If len(word) > 10, abbreviate. If len(word) <= 10, print the word unchanged.

observations.title: Compute the middle count
observations.description: The abbreviation replaces all characters between the first and last with their count. The count is len(word) - 2.

hints.nudge: How many letters are between the first and last characters?
hints.question: If the word has length L, and you keep the first and last characters, how many characters are in between?
hints.formal: The middle count is len(word) - 2. The abbreviation is word[0] + str(len(word) - 2) + word[-1].

observations.title: Construct the output string
observations.description: Concatenate the first character, the middle count as a string, and the last character to form the abbreviation.

hints.nudge: You have three pieces to glue together. What are they?
hints.question: How do you build the final abbreviated string from the first character, the number, and the last character?
